title: HTML Link Semantics, Base URLs, Relations, and Discovery Decisions · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in semantic link-discovery ledger and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Enumerate link-bearing HTML elements and distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, form, media, and embedded destinations.
Apply first-valid-base rules against the final response URL and retain base source and rejected alternatives.
Interpret rel tokens, download, target, media, hreflang, canonical, alternate, and nofollow under an explicit discovery policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Link Bearing Elements
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 7440 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify link-bearing elements, base and relation semantics, rejected references, and discovery provenance; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate link-bearing HTML elements and distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, form, media, and embedded destinations.

Inspect the Link Bearing Elements trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Link Bearing Elements.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Link Bearing Elements, keep Link Bearing Elements connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enumerate link-bearing HTML elements and distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, form, media, and embedded destinations. Artifact under review: semantic link-discovery ledger.

options.text: The Link Bearing Elements record says semantic link-discovery ledger looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Link Bearing Elements.

For inspect the failure record, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Link Bearing Elements for semantic link-discovery ledger.
The record names the assumption, applies Link Bearing Elements, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
